behavior_summary: Handles player inspection ('U8P_CYRRUS::look') of the NPC Cyrrus and emits a short context-sensitive description or bark depending on whether Cyrrus is dead and on two global flags (cyrrusName, cyrrusJob).
Chooses and stores a runtime schedule (cyrrusSchedule) via a schedule routine that queries world/time state (FREE::28F9) and then spawns schedule-specific behavior routines.

Responds to the Avatar entering/leaving Cyrrus' fast-trigger area: when entering it either teleports a dead Cyrrus to random coordinates or resets references and spawns a behavior routine determined by cyrrusSchedule; leaving clears cyrrusJustHealed and resets refs.

Implements a cast ritual that waits for a target, performs dialog/animation/sfx, clears immortality and sets Cyrrus dead, then teleports the NPC (inferred scripted self-ritual that ends in death/relocation).
Reacts to theft by scanning items, possibly pathfinding to an item location, barking at the player, and spawning follow-up reaction behaviors (schedule-dependent).
Uses resetRef often to clear script references before starting new behaviors; spawns many engine methods (METHOD::* and FREE::*) to schedule animations, timers and auxiliary effects.
notable_control_flow: CYRRUS::look (main entry) first checks Npc::isDead; if true emits 'the late Cyrrus ' bark; otherwise reads global booleans cyrrusName and cyrrusJob to pick one of several alive descriptions ('Holy Cyrrus, the healer', 'Cyrrus', 'monk', 'man').

CYRRUS::schedule performs multiple FREE::28F9 queries and uses their results to set a local choice ([BP-09h]) which is then written to global cyrrusSchedule and drives spawning of schedule-specific methods.

U8P_CYRRUS::enterFastArea: if Npc::isDead -> choose random x/y via urandom and call Npc::teleport; else -> call resetRef for several refs and spawn a schedule-specific behavior (U8P_CYRRUS::05B3, U8P_CYRRUS::0D97 or U8P_CYRRUS::1357 depending on cyrrusSchedule).
U8P_CYRRUS::leaveFastArea clears cyrrusJustHealed (writes 0) and calls resetRef for the same set of refs as enterFastArea.

U8P_CYRRUS::cast loops polling Npc::getTarget and yields via FREE::1C19 until a target exists; upon a match it resets refs, barks 'Aid me, Stratos!', spawns effect/timer routines (FREE::29D8, METHOD::15E7) and proceeds through an animation/effects sequence that ends with Npc::clrImmortal and Npc::setDead followed by Npc::teleport.

U8P_CYRRUS::AvatarStoleSomething scans player/item lists in a loop, sets NPC activity/pathfind to an item location and may bark at the player before spawning enterFastArea behavior; some branches short-circuit based on cyrrusSchedule.
Loop/frame progression inside U8P_CYRRUS::schedule; treated as control-flow, not explicit internal call.
open_questions: Exact text-to-flag mapping: which specific U8P_CYRRUS::look strings correspond to the true/false combinations of cyrrusName and cyrrusJob (the general options are known but precise mapping is inferred).

Precise semantics and return values of FREE::28F9 used during scheduling (time-of-day/calendar queries vs other world-state checks) — schedule selection logic is inferred from comparisons but not fully known.
Detailed behavior of spawned METHOD::* routines (METHOD::143A, METHOD::133F, METHOD::15E7, etc.) — their exact animation/movement/timer roles are inferred from usage but not explicit.
Why the cast sequence marks Cyrrus dead and teleports him — narrative intent is inferred (ritual/sacrifice/heal) but not explicit in the code excerpt.
Exact role and lifetime semantics of cyrrusJustHealed (beyond leaveFastArea clearing it) and interactions with other scripts not present in this file.
Concrete meanings of several local temporaries (full set of BP-offsets) beyond the main ones used for schedule selection and loop counters.

main_function.name: U8P_CYRRUS::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a player's 'U8P_CYRRUS::look' (examine) event for the NPC Cyrrus by emitting a short context-aware bark/description depending on whether Cyrrus is dead and on global flags that control name/job presentation.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player (or an actor) performs an examine/U8P_CYRRUS::look event on the Cyrrus NPC (Event 0).
Runs in the NPC's process context and expects a pid parameter (viewer) and the NPC object pointer (implicit).

function_map.name: U8P_CYRRUS::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_CYRRUS::look' event and select/display an appropriate short description/bark for Cyrrus (alive vs dead and name/job variants).

function_map.name: U8P_CYRRUS::schedule
function_map.role: Determine and write a schedule value to global cyrrusSchedule by querying world/time state (FREE::28F9) and spawn follow-up behavior methods.

function_map.name: U8P_CYRRUS::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: teleport a dead Cyrrus randomly; otherwise reset refs and spawn schedule-specific behavior routines based on cyrrusSchedule.

function_map.name: U8P_CYRRUS::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit trigger: clear cyrrusJustHealed and reset script references to clean up running behaviors.

function_map.name: U8P_CYRRUS::cast
function_map.role: Handle a casting event: wait for/inspect a target, perform dialog, spawn visual/sound/timer effects and run an animation sequence that clears immortality and sets Cyrrus dead, then teleports him.

function_map.name: U8P_CYRRUS::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: React to theft: optionally ignore based on schedule, otherwise clear refs, search items, pathfind to an item, bark at the player, and trigger follow-up behavior (enterFastArea).

function_map.name: U8P_CYRRUS::05B3
function_map.role: Schedule-specific behavior routine (spawned when cyrrusSchedule==1): handles animation/frame checks, item scanning, pathfinding and meditative/dialog behavior loops.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Engine query to determine if the NPC is dead; used to alter dialog/behavior flows.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit a short on-screen dialog/description (NPC or item 'bark') toward a pid; used for all short lines in U8P_CYRRUS::look() and cast().

function_map.name: U8P_CYRRUS::resetRef
function_map.role: Clear stored script/object references for the NPC context to cancel or reset running sub-behaviors.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Produce a random word less than a given bound; used to pick random teleport coordinates for a dead NPC.

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: Teleport the NPC to supplied coordinates/floor/facing; used to relocate Cyrrus (notably when dead).

internal_calls.purpose: Check if Cyrrus is dead to decide whether to use the 'late' description or alive variants.
Emit the chosen short description to the player/process that looked at Cyrrus.
Spawn schedule-specific behavior when cyrrusSchedule == 1 (one of the branches spawned on area entry).
Spawn schedule-specific behavior when cyrrusSchedule == 2 (another branch on area entry).
Spawn schedule-specific behavior when cyrrusSchedule == 3 (a third branch on area entry).
After reacting to theft (bark/pathfind), spawn enterFastArea behavior to pursue or otherwise continue reaction.

external_calls.purpose: Query dead/alive state to select dialog; prevents alive-only descriptions for dead NPCs.
Display the short description string to the player who looked at Cyrrus.
Query world/time/state conditions used to select a schedule index (cyrrusSchedule).
Pick random map coordinates when teleporting a dead Cyrrus.
Relocate Cyrrus to randomly chosen coordinates (used when Cyrrus is dead upon area entry).
Poll for the NPC's current target; used in a retry loop to wait until a valid target exists before executing the cast sequence.
Yield/sleep during target-polling retries to avoid tight loops while waiting for a target.
Spawn a timed visual/sound effect as part of the cast sequence.
Remove immortality flag from Cyrrus as part of the ritual/cast sequence (precedes setting dead).
Mark Cyrrus as dead during the cast/ritual sequence; likely ends the NPC's active life state.
Request pathfinding toward an item or location detected by the theft-handling scan loop.
Speak a reproach/alert to the player after detecting theft-related conditions.

reads.name: cyrrusName
reads.likely_effect: Influences the text used in CYRRUS::look to choose which name/label to display for Cyrrus (affects whether to use 'Holy Cyrrus' vs 'Cyrrus' etc.).

reads.name: cyrrusJob
reads.likely_effect: Combined with cyrrusName when alive to choose a job-related descriptor (e.g., 'the healer' or 'monk' vs 'man').

reads.name: cyrrusSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Selects which schedule-specific behavior routine is spawned on fast-area entry and by theft reaction logic (read by enterFastArea and others).

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: Checked before spawning speech/behavior to avoid interrupting other dialog (used as a guard to suppress barks/spawns).

writes.name: cyrrusSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Stored by U8P_CYRRUS::schedule to persist the chosen schedule index for later behavior selection on area entry and other events.

writes.name: cyrrusJustHealed
writes.likely_effect: Cleared (set to 0) by U8P_CYRRUS::leaveFastArea to indicate the NPC is no longer in a recently-healed state; likely prevents immediate re-triggering of heal behavior.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles player inspection ('U8P_CYRRUS::look') of the NPC Cyrrus and emits a short context-sensitive description or bark depending on whether Cyrrus is dead and on two global flags (cyrrusName, cyrrusJob).
